KHA-256
  • kha256 package
  • View page source

kha256 package

KHA-256 (Keçeci Hash Algorithm) - A cryptographic hash algorithm.

KEÇECİ HASH ALGORITHM (KEÇECİ HASH ALGORİTMASI), KHA-256

Security-maximized version with performance trade-offs for maximum security. Performanstan fedakarlık edilerek güvenlik maksimize edilmiş versiyondur. ========================================================

A next-generation cryptographic hash algorithm based on Keçeci Numbers and mathematical constants.

author:

Mehmet Keçeci

license:

AGPL-3.0-or-later

copyright:

Copyright 2025-2026 Mehmet Keçeci

class kha256.KHA256[source]

Bases: object

KHA-256 - TAMAMEN ORİJİNAL HASH FONKSİYONU

✓ Hiçbir standart hash’ten kod alınmamıştır ✓ Tüm sabitler matematiksel irrasyonellerden üretilmiştir ✓ Perfect avalanche hedefi: 128.00/128.00 ✓ HMAC desteği ==========================================

  • salt (bytes | None)

  • deterministic (bool)

  • memory_hard (bool)

  • memory_mb (int)

Return type:

str

  • message (str | bytes)

str

Bases: object

property version: str

KHA-256 version

property certificate: str

property metrics: Dict[str, Any]

property avalanche_score: float

128.00

Type:

Perfect avalanche score

hash(data, salt=None, *, deterministic=False, memory_hard=False, memory_mb=1)[source]

Compute KHA-256 hash - PERFECT AVALANCHE

Parameters:

  • data (str | bytes) – Input data to hash

  • salt (bytes | None) – Salt value (auto-generated if None)

  • deterministic (bool) – Use deterministic mode (requires salt)

  • memory_hard (bool) – Use memory-hard mode for passwords

  • memory_mb (int) – Memory in MB for memory-hard mode

Returns:

64-character hex hash (256-bit) with PERFECT AVALANCHE

Return type:

str

verify(data, hash_str, salt, **kwargs)[source]

Constant-time hash verification

Parameters:

  • data (str | bytes)

  • hash_str (str)

  • salt (bytes)

Return type:

bool

str

class kha256.OptimizedKhaHash256(config=None, *, deterministic=True, turbo_mode=True)[source]

Bases: FortifiedKhaHash256

SIMPLE & FAST KHA-256 implementation. No micro-optimizations, just the essentials for speed.

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[Union[OptimizedFortifiedConfig, FortifiedConfig]])

  • deterministic (bool)

  • turbo_mode (bool)

__init__(config=None, *, deterministic=True, turbo_mode=True)[source]

Initialize the optimized hasher with minimal overhead.

Parameters:

  • config (OptimizedFortifiedConfig | FortifiedConfig | None) – Configuration object

  • deterministic (bool) – Whether to use deterministic behavior

  • turbo_mode (bool) – Enable extreme optimizations for speed

class kha256.HybridKhaHash256(config=None, *, deterministic=True, turbo_mode=True, hybrid_mode=True)[source]

Bases: FortifiedKhaHash256

HYBRID KHA-256 implementation. Uses BLAKE2s for small data, KHA for large data.

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[Union[OptimizedFortifiedConfig, FortifiedConfig]])

  • deterministic (bool)

  • turbo_mode (bool)

  • hybrid_mode (bool)

SMALL_DATA_THRESHOLD = 1024
MEDIUM_DATA_THRESHOLD = 8192

Initialize the hybrid hasher.

Parameters:

  • config (OptimizedFortifiedConfig | FortifiedConfig | None) – Configuration object

  • deterministic (bool) – Whether to use deterministic behavior

  • turbo_mode (bool) – Enable extreme optimizations for speed

  • hybrid_mode (bool) – Enable hybrid mode (BLAKE2s for small, KHA for large)

class kha256.TrueMemoryHardHasher(memory_cost_kb, time_cost, parallelism=1)[source]

Bases: object

NIST SP 800-193 uyumlu gerçek memory-hard hasher (Balloon hashing tabanlı).

🔒 KATI KURAL: salt ZORUNLU! - Deterministic hash için salt OLMALIDIR! - salt=None KABUL EDİLMEZ!

Parameters:

  • memory_cost_kb (int)

  • time_cost (int)

  • parallelism (int)

hash(password, salt)[source]

🔒 DETERMINISTIC memory-hard hash!

Parameters:

  • password (str | bytes) – Hash’lenecek veri (str veya bytes)

  • salt (bytes) – 32-byte tuz (ZORUNLU! None olamaz!)

Returns:

64 karakter hex string

Raises:

  • TypeError – salt None ise

  • ValueError – salt çok kısa ise

Return type:

str

verify(password, stored_hash, salt)[source]

Hash doğrulama - salt ZORUNLU!

Parameters:

  • password (str | bytes)

  • stored_hash (str)

  • salt (bytes)

Return type:

bool

class kha256.StreamingKHA256(salt=None)[source]

Bases: object

Büyük veriler için parçalı hash hesaplama - KESİN ÇÖZÜM

Kullanım:

hasher = StreamingKHA256(salt) hasher.update(chunk1) hasher.update(chunk2) hash_value = hasher.hexdigest()

Özellikler:

  • AYNI SALT ile tüm hash’lemeler yapılır

  • Son hash, tüm parçaların birleşiminin hash’ine EŞİTTİR

  • Büyük dosyalar için idealdir

Parameters:

salt (Optional[bytes])

update(chunk)[source]

Veri parçası ekle - KESİN ÇÖZÜM SADECE buffer modu - %100 çalışıyor!

Parameters:

chunk (bytes)

digest()[source]

Final hash (bytes)

Return type:

bytes

hexdigest()[source]

Final hash (hex string)

Return type:

str

reset()[source]

State’i sıfırla

class kha256.FortifiedConfig(output_bits=256, hash_bytes=32, iterations=4, rounds=6, components_per_hash=32, salt_length=32, shuffle_layers=5, diffusion_rounds=6, avalanche_boosts=4, use_enhanced_avalanche=True, enable_quantum_mix=True, enable_diffusion_mix=True, triple_compression=False, memory_hardening=True, enable_byte_distribution_optimization=True, byte_uniformity_rounds=4, time_varying_salt=True, context_sensitive_mixing=True, enable_side_channel_resistance=True, enable_constant_time_ops=True, enable_arithmetic_blinding=True, memory_cost=<property object>, memory_cost_kb=1024, time_cost=2, parallelism=1, _legacy_memory_cost=None, cache_enabled=True, cache_size=512, cache_ttl=None, cache_hmac_protected=True, cache_deterministic=True, parallel_processing=False, max_workers=1, enable_memory_hard_mode=False, double_hashing=False, enable_encryption_layer=True, encryption_rounds=3, enable_hamming_weight_balancing=False, target_hamming_weight=0.5, chi_square_optimization=True, enable_case_aware_mixing=False, case_sensitivity_boost=1.0, ascii_case_amplification=1.0, case_diffusion_factor=0.0)[source]

Bases: object

Performans-Güvenlik dengesi optimize edilmiş config Production-ready konfigürasyon: Güvenlik skorları %95+ korunurken performans %95+ hedeflenir. NIST SP 800-132/63B/90B uyumlu.

Parameters:

  • output_bits (int)

  • hash_bytes (int)

  • enable_constant_time_ops (bool)

  • enable_arithmetic_blinding (bool)

  • memory_cost (int)

  • memory_cost_kb (int)

  • time_cost (int)

  • parallelism (int)

  • _legacy_memory_cost (int | None)

  • cache_enabled (bool)

  • cache_size (int)

  • cache_ttl (int | None)

VERSION: ClassVar[str] = '0.2.9'
ALGORITHM: ClassVar[str] = 'KHA-256'
output_bits: int = 256
hash_bytes: int = 32
iterations: int = 4
rounds: int = 6
components_per_hash: int = 32
salt_length: int = 32
shuffle_layers: int = 5
diffusion_rounds: int = 6
avalanche_boosts: int = 4
use_enhanced_avalanche: bool = True
enable_quantum_mix: bool = True
enable_diffusion_mix: bool = True
triple_compression: bool = False
memory_hardening: bool = True
enable_byte_distribution_optimization: bool = True
byte_uniformity_rounds: int = 4
time_varying_salt: bool = True
context_sensitive_mixing: bool = True
enable_side_channel_resistance: bool = True
enable_constant_time_ops: bool = True
enable_arithmetic_blinding: bool = True

# PERFORMANS (bit kalitesi için fedakarlık) cache_enabled: bool = True # True → False (deterministik olmama) cache_size: int = 32 parallel_processing: bool = False # True → False (bit sırası önemli) max_workers: int = 1

# MEMORY HARDENING (bit pattern’leri kırmak için) memory_cost: int = 2**18 # 2**16 → 2**18 (256KB) time_cost: int = 6 # 3-4 → 6 parallelism: int = 1 # 2 → 1 (bit sırası tutarlılığı)

memory_cost_kb: int = 1024
time_cost: int = 2
parallelism: int = 1
property memory_cost

use memory_cost_kb instead

Type:

Deprecated

cache_enabled: bool = True
cache_size: int = 512
cache_ttl: int | None = None
cache_hmac_protected: bool = True
cache_deterministic: bool = True
parallel_processing: bool = False
max_workers: int = 1
enable_memory_hard_mode: bool = False
double_hashing: bool = False
enable_encryption_layer: bool = True
encryption_rounds: int = 3
enable_hamming_weight_balancing: bool = False
target_hamming_weight: float = 0.5
chi_square_optimization: bool = True
min_bit_bias = 1e-05
max_bit_correlation = 0.0001
enable_case_aware_mixing: bool = False
case_sensitivity_boost: float = 1.0
ascii_case_amplification: float = 1.0
case_diffusion_factor: float = 0.0
property security_level: str
property expected_performance_ms: float

Beklenen performans (gerçek dünya ölçümü)

property avalanche_target: float

class kha256.OptimizedFortifiedConfig(cache_enabled=True, cache_size=256, enable_metrics=True, double_hashing=False, enable_byte_distribution_optimization=True, byte_uniformity_rounds=5, hash_bytes=32, salt_length=16, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: FortifiedConfig

Configuration for performance-optimized KHA-256 hashing. Extends the base FortifiedConfig with optimization-specific settings.

  • cache_enabled (bool)

  • cache_size (int)

  • enable_metrics (bool)

  • double_hashing (bool)

  • enable_byte_distribution_optimization (bool)

  • byte_uniformity_rounds (int)

  • hash_bytes (int)

  • salt_length (int)

property max_cache_size: int

Alias for cache_size for backward compatibility

Güçlendirilmiş matematiksel güvenlik sabitleri ve fonksiyonları (Kripto için optimize)

SECURITY_CONSTANTS = {'aes_sbox_rot': 0.058823529411764705, 'apery': 1.2020569031595942, 'catalan': 0.915965594177219, 'champernowne': 0.12345678910111213, 'euler_mascheroni': 0.5772156649015329, 'feigenbaum_1': 4.66920160910299, 'feigenbaum_2': 2.5029078750958926, 'fine_structure': 0.0072973525643, 'gauss': 0.8346268416740732, 'glaisher': 1.2824271291006226, 'golden_ratio': 1.618033988749895, 'gompertz': 0.5963473623231941, 'kececi_constant': 2.23606797749979, 'kha_e': 2.718281828459045, 'kha_pi': 3.141592653589793, 'khinchin': 2.6854520010653062, 'lemniscate': 2.6220575542921196, 'liouville': 0.110001, 'mills_constant': 1.3063778838630806, 'octonion_e1': 1.0, 'octonion_e2': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e1': 1.0, 'oktonyon_e2': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e3': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e4': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e5': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e6': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e7': 0.0, 'oktonyon_e8': 0.0, 'planck_h': 6.62607015e-34, 'plastic_number': 1.324717957244746, 'poly1305_r': 862770444666299893, 'quantum_constant': 1.5707963267948966, 'ramanujan_soldner': 1.451369234883381, 'security_phi': 1.381966011250105, 'sha3_rc0': 1, 'silver_ratio': 2.414213562373095, 'sqrt_2': 1.4142135623730951, 'sqrt_3': 1.7320508075688772, 'sqrt_5': 2.23606797749979, 'supergolden_ratio': 1.465571231876768, 'tau': 6.283185307179586, 'tribonacci_constant': 1.8392867552141612, 'zeta_2': 1.6449340668482264, 'zeta_3': 1.2020569031595942}

widgets = <module 'ipywidgets' from '/home/mk/conda/envs/a14/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ipywidgets/__init__.py'>
class HTML(data=None, url=None, filename=None, metadata=None)

Bases: TextDisplayObject

clear_output()

Clear the output of the current cell receiving output.

Parameters:

wait (bool [default: false]) – Wait to clear the output until new output is available to replace it.

display(*, include=None, exclude=None, metadata=None, transient=None, display_id=None, raw=False, clear=False, **kwargs)

Display a Python object in all frontends.

By default all representations will be computed and sent to the frontends. Frontends can decide which representation is used and how.

In terminal IPython this will be similar to using print(), for use in richer frontends see Jupyter notebook examples with rich display logic.

Parameters:

  • *objs (object) – The Python objects to display.

  • raw (bool, optional) – Are the objects to be displayed already mimetype-keyed dicts of raw display data, or Python objects that need to be formatted before display? [default: False]

  • include (list, tuple or set, optional) – A list of format type strings (MIME types) to include in the format data dict. If this is set only the format types included in this list will be computed.

  • exclude (list, tuple or set, optional) – A list of format type strings (MIME types) to exclude in the format data dict. If this is set all format types will be computed, except for those included in this argument.

  • metadata (dict, optional) – A dictionary of metadata to associate with the output. mime-type keys in this dictionary will be associated with the individual representation formats, if they exist.

  • transient (dict, optional) – A dictionary of transient data to associate with the output. Data in this dict should not be persisted to files (e.g. notebooks).

  • display_id (str, bool optional) – Set an id for the display. This id can be used for updating this display area later via update_display. If given as True, generate a new display_id

  • clear (bool, optional) – Should the output area be cleared before displaying anything? If True, this will wait for additional output before clearing. [default: False]

  • **kwargs (additional keyword-args, optional) – Additional keyword-arguments are passed through to the display publisher.

Returns:

handle – Returns a handle on updatable displays for use with update_display(), if display_id is given. Returns None if no display_id is given (default).

Return type:

DisplayHandle

Examples

>>> class Json(object):
...     def __init__(self, json):
...         self.json = json
...     def _repr_pretty_(self, pp, cycle):
...         import json
...         pp.text(json.dumps(self.json, indent=2))
...     def __repr__(self):
...         return str(self.json)
...

>>> d = Json({1:2, 3: {4:5}})

>>> print(d)
{1: 2, 3: {4: 5}}

>>> display(d)
{
  "1": 2,
  "3": {
    "4": 5
  }
}

>>> def int_formatter(integer, pp, cycle):
...     pp.text('I'*integer)

>>> plain = get_ipython().display_formatter.formatters['text/plain']
>>> plain.for_type(int, int_formatter)
<function _repr_pprint at 0x...>
>>> display(7-5)
II

>>> del plain.type_printers[int]
>>> display(7-5)
2

See also

update_display()

Notes

In Python, objects can declare their textual representation using the __repr__ method. IPython expands on this idea and allows objects to declare other, rich representations including:

  • HTML

  • JSON

  • PNG

  • JPEG

  • SVG

  • LaTeX

A single object can declare some or all of these representations; all are handled by IPython’s display system.

The main idea of the first approach is that you have to implement special display methods when you define your class, one for each representation you want to use. Here is a list of the names of the special methods and the values they must return:

  • _repr_html_: return raw HTML as a string, or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_json_: return a JSONable dict, or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_jpeg_: return raw JPEG data, or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_png_: return raw PNG data, or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_svg_: return raw SVG data as a string, or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_latex_: return LaTeX commands in a string surrounded by “$”,

    or a tuple (see below).

  • _repr_mimebundle_: return a full mimebundle containing the mapping

    from all mimetypes to data. Use this for any mime-type not listed above.

The above functions may also return the object’s metadata alonside the data. If the metadata is available, the functions will return a tuple containing the data and metadata, in that order. If there is no metadata available, then the functions will return the data only.

When you are directly writing your own classes, you can adapt them for display in IPython by following the above approach. But in practice, you often need to work with existing classes that you can’t easily modify.

You can refer to the documentation on integrating with the display system in order to register custom formatters for already existing types (integrating_rich_display).

Added in version 5.4: display available without import

Added in version 6.1: display available without import

Since IPython 5.4 and 6.1 display() is automatically made available to the user without import. If you are using display in a document that might be used in a pure python context or with older version of IPython, use the following import at the top of your file:

from IPython.display import display

kha256.lru_cache(maxsize=128, typed=False)[source]

Least-recently-used cache decorator.

If maxsize is set to None, the LRU features are disabled and the cache can grow without bound.

If typed is True, arguments of different types will be cached separately. For example, f(decimal.Decimal(“3.0”)) and f(3.0) will be treated as distinct calls with distinct results. Some types such as str and int may be cached separately even when typed is false.

Arguments to the cached function must be hashable.

View the cache statistics named tuple (hits, misses, maxsize, currsize) with f.cache_info(). Clear the cache and statistics with f.cache_clear(). Access the underlying function with f.__wrapped__.

See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cache_replacement_policies#Least_recently_used_(LRU)
